taskDescription: Click on the key anatomical terms related to the face and parotid region in the text below.
textField:
The muscles of *facial* expression are all derived from the second *pharyngeal* arch and innervated by the *facial* nerve (CN *VII*). The nerve exits the skull through the *stylomastoid* foramen and enters the *parotid* gland, where it divides into five *terminal* branches: *temporal*, *zygomatic*, *buccal*, *marginal* *mandibular*, and *cervical*.

In an *upper* *motor* *neuron* lesion, the *forehead* is spared because the upper part of the facial nucleus receives *bilateral* *cortical* input. In a *lower* motor neuron lesion, the entire ipsilateral face is paralysed.

The *parotid* duct (*Stensen's* duct) crosses the *masseter*, pierces the *buccinator*, and opens opposite the upper second *molar*. The *retromandibular* vein and *external* *carotid* artery traverse the gland. The *pterygoid* venous plexus communicates via the deep facial vein, creating a dangerous pathway to the *cavernous* *sinus*.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review face and parotid anatomy and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent understanding of the face and parotid region!
